evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC layout directly fulfills brief; diagonal reference line and prevalence baseline present; AUROC/AP annotated in legends; all four classifiers clearly distinguishable.
evidence.typography: pdffonts shows Type 3 (bitmap-path) DejaVu Sans fonts — not Type 42/TrueType; script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default, which is explicitly flagged.
evidence.palette: Four distinct, colorblind-friendly colors used (grey, blue, red-orange, green); both color and linestyle encode method identity redundantly, which aids accessibility; green is used for best-performing method (directionally appropriate), not for a generic category.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout used; axes span nearly the full figure; y-range -0.02–1.02 is sensible for probability curves; no obvious excessive whitespace or label overlaps.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Legends placed at lower-right (ROC) and lower-left (PRC) avoiding curve clutter; caption matches figure content; random-classifier label in ROC legend includes AUC=0.500 — minor: legend entry for random classifier may look noisy in PRC panel.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts reports Type 3 (not selectable/editable TrueType); script does not set matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42; text is not selectable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial (data.json is missing from listing but the script ran successfully).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states exact sample sizes (n+=120, n-=180), prevalence (0.400), names all methods with ranked AUROC/AP values, and identifies OursModel as best with specific numeric claims (AUROC=0.989, AP=0.984).

overall_strengths: Redundant encoding (color + linestyle) makes the figure accessible to colorblind readers and printable in grayscale.
Caption is quantitatively precise, naming all four methods with their AUROC and AP scores and providing sample sizes and prevalence.
overall_weaknesses: Fonts are Type 3 (bitmap outlines) rather than Type 42 (embedded TrueType); pdf.fonttype=42 is not set, so text is not selectable or searchable in the PDF.
Default DejaVu Sans typeface is used throughout — a journal-ready figure should use a font matching the manuscript (e.g., Times, Helvetica, or a sans-serif matching the template).
